channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How I’m Building SEO + GEO for CandleDojo, a Chart-Reading Trainer
item.description: Hi there! I’ve been working on CandleDojo, a browser-based chart-reading trainer for traders. The product idea was always pretty clear in my head: help traders get structured reps reading...

item.title: How to Self-Host n8n in 2026: VPS vs Managed Hosting (Full Comparison)
item.description: Liquid syntax error: Unknown tag 'hint'

item.title: Compile-Time vs Run-Time Exceptions in Java
item.description: What is a Compile-Time Exception? A compile-time exception (also called a checked exception) is an error that occurs before the program runs, during compilation. The Java compiler checks these...

item.title: What Broke When We Pushed WebSockets From 100k to 1M Users
item.description: A post-mortem on OOM kills, GC pauses, and the slow consumers that ate our RAM. What Broke When We Pushed WebSockets From 100k to 1M Users A post-mortem on OOM kills, GC pauses, and the slow...

item.title: The latest Matter update improves camera streaming
item.description: The Connectivity Standards Alliance (CSA) released Matter 1.5.1 today, building upon the camera and video doorbell support added as part of the Matter 1.5 update last November. Although 1.5.1 is a...

item.title: The Web Was Built for Humans. AI Is Struggling Because of It.
item.description: AI is getting better at reasoning. It is still surprisingly bad at using software. That gap is not because the models are weak. It is because the web was never designed to be legible to machines...

item.title: How to Engineer a Single Backend Server for 5M Concurrent Connections
item.description: The invisible cost of scale isn’t your code — it’s every assumption the operating system makes about what “normal” looks like. How to Engineer a Single Backend Server for 5M Concurrent Connections...

item.title: Python Developer AI Toolkit, Part 1: How I stopped rewriting the same prompts and packaged 272 that actually work
item.description: Every time I started a Python code review or backend debugging session, I'd spend 10-15 minutes writing the prompt. Not the review — the prompt. Then I'd get inconsistent output, tweak the prompt,...

item.title: Implementing a RAG system: Walk
item.description: Now that we've established the basics in our "Crawl" phase, it's time to pick up the pace. In this guid, we'll move beyond the initial setup to focus on optimizing core architectural components...

item.title: How We Built Edge-First License Validation in Under 50ms
item.description: TL;DR We built a licensing API where license validation happens at the CDN edge - not a centralized server. The result: sub-50ms validation times globally. The Problem Most licensing APIs work...

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Game Engine Lock-in (and What Web Devs Can Learn)
item.description: A game that earns $10M on Unreal Engine pays $450,000 in royalties. The same game on Godot pays $0. That's a real calculation from the GDC 2026 data, not a hypothetical. Game developers have been...

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Attacks on Southeast Asian Government Networks
item.description: A high-severity security flaw in the TrueConf client video conferencing software has been exploited in the wild as a zero-day as part of a campaign targeting government entities in Southeast Asia...

item.title: The best deals to shop on the last day of Amazon’s Big Spring Sale
item.description: Amazon loves to manufacture an event. March is historically a dry spell for deals; however, with Amazon’s third annual Big Spring Sale, which runs through 11:59PM PT tonight, the retail behemoth...

item.title: Meta launches new ‘prescription optimized’ smart glasses
item.description: While Meta has released several versions of its AI glasses over the last few years, today it announced it's released two new styles specifically geared toward those of us with less-than-perfect...

item.title: Sony’s WH-1000XM5 headphones are a whopping 39 percent off
item.description: Amazon is hosting a fantastic lightning sale on every color of the Sony WH-1000XM5 over-ear headphones, offering them for $243 (originally $399) for a limited time. This is one of the biggest...

item.title: TCL is taking over Sony’s TV business
item.description: After signing a nonbinding agreement in January to spin off its TV business, today Sony officially announced that TCL will pay approximately 75.4 billion yen (over $473 million) for a 51 percent...

item.title: Granite 4.0 3B Vision: Compact Multimodal Intelligence for Enterprise Documents
Nomadic raises $8.4 million to wrangle the data pouring off autonomous vehicles
item.description: The company turns footage from robots into structured, searchable datasets with a deep learning model.

item.title: Razer’s first split ergonomic keyboard puts extra keys in reach of your thumbs
item.description: Razer has announced its first keyboard with a split and curved ergonomic layout. The new Pro Type Ergo is available starting today, which at $189.99 is slightly more expensive than the more...

item.title: Art schools are being torn apart by AI
item.description: When my baby brother, a 3D modelling and animation student, talks to me about his projects and studies, the pride I usually feel is becoming increasingly tainted by a growing sense of dread. As a...

item.title: Meta agrees to ‘reduce’ Instagram’s PG-13 rating references
item.description: Meta has reached an agreement with the Motion Picture Association (MPA) that will require the platform to "substantially reduce" its reference to the PG-13 rating when describing Instagram's teen...

item.title: Shifting to AI model customization is an architectural imperative
item.description: In the early days of large language models (LLMs), we grew accustomed to massive 10x jumps in reasoning and coding capability with every new model iteration. Today, those jumps have flattened into...

item.title: OkCupid settles claims it shared user photos with a facial recognition company
item.description: Dating app OkCupid agreed to settle claims from the Federal Trade Commission that it deceived millions of users by sharing their photos with a third-party facial recognition company without their...

item.title: Instagram tests letting you pay to view stories anonymously
item.description: Meta is testing an Instagram subscription that offers a bunch of new perks, such as the ability to view stories without the poster knowing, according to a report from TechCrunch. The paid plan...

item.title: Exclusive: Runway launches $10M fund, Builders program to support early-stage AI startups
item.description: Runway is launching a $10 million fund and startup program to back companies building with its AI video models, as it pushes toward interactive, real-time “video intelligence” applications.

item.title: Vertex AI Vulnerability Exposes Google Cloud Data and Private Artifacts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a security "blind spot" in Google Cloud's Vertex AI platform that could allow artificial intelligence (AI) agents to be weaponized by an attacker to gain...

item.title: With its new app store, Ring bets on AI to go beyond home security
item.description: Ring's app store will allow the company to target broader use cases beyond security, like elder care or business needs.

item.title: AI Has Flooded All the Weather Apps
item.description: Weather forecasting has gotten a big boost from machine learning. How that translates into what users see can vary.

item.title: The Download: AI health tools and the Pentagon’s Anthropic culture war
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. There are more AI health tools than ever—but how well do...
